div.head: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom (Winant) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 2646. For SayreFrancis B. Sayre, Deputy Director, Office of Foreign Relief and Rehabilitation Operations. from Lehman. Jackson advised Gore-Booth that proposed $2,500,000 fund was assumed by us to be used for the movement of refugees from Spain to other countries. This proposal was originally put forth by LongBreckinridge Long, Assistant Secretary of State. and Gore-Booth was advised to consult Brandt, since question of movement of refugees being handled by that office instead of O.F.R.R.O.Office of Foreign Relief and Rehabilitation Operations. We believe that consideration of the establishment of any such fund should be discussed by Bermuda Conference. [Lehman.]
